context.description: A philosophical nonfiction chapter section about the body as a machine and consciousness as a passenger. It uses a staged example of exercise and an emergency attack to contrast ordinary exhaustion with extreme survival-driven capability under the heading "Die Faust des Riesen."
normalization_notes: German prose translated into English control-language values.
Section heading preserved from visible text.

rows.paragraph_text: The body is described as a system with fixed needs, while consciousness is framed as a passenger choosing between survival and failure.
The section "Die Faust des Riesen" presents a scenario in which coordinated bodily systems produce extraordinary strength and focus during an emergency.
A training example escalates into a violent attack, showing how exhaustion disappears and survival response enables rapid, decisive action.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content chapter_section Book page from Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2, page 30, section "Die Faust des Riesen." Philosophical nonfiction prose about consciousness, the body, exercise, and emergency survival response.
